questions.question: Does buildImetaTag(attachment, role) return a string[] whose first element is 'imeta', and includes entries for url, m, x, size, dim, blurhash, filename, n, v, and role when all fields are populated per AC-10?
Does parseMediaImetaTag(buildImetaTag(attachment, 'full')) return a MediaAttachment whose url, sha256, type, filename, nonce, and version equal the original attachment per AC-11?
Does buildImetaTag(attachment, 'thumb') produce a tag containing 'role thumb', and buildImetaTag(attachment, 'full') produce a tag containing 'role full' per AC-12?
Does buildImageMessageContent(caption) return a JSON string that JSON.parse decodes to { type: 'image', version: 1, caption } per AC-13?
Does buildImageMessageContent('') produce caption: '', and does parseImageMessageContent applied to that string return { type: 'image', version: 1, caption: '' } per AC-14?
Does parseImageMessageContent return null for non-JSON, for type !== 'image', for missing version, and for existing poll_open/poll_close discriminators per AC-15?
Does extractAttachmentsByRole correctly identify full vs thumb by reading the 'role <value>' entry within each imeta tag, treating missing role as 'full' per spec?
Do the 24 unit tests (8 for imetaTag, 16 for imageMessage) all pass in Node environment without any browser API stubs required?
Are all imports from @internet-privacy/marmot-ts using the package root (not deep subpaths like /dist/core/media.js) to comply with the package's exports map?
Is imetaTag.ts a hand-rolled NIP-92 serialiser (no applesauce-common dependency) as required by the spec to avoid adding the entire applesauce stack?

answers.evidence: buildImetaTag() returns ['imeta'] at imetaTag.ts:4 with entries for url, m, x, size (optional), dim (optional), blurhash (optional), filename, n, v, role. Test imetaTag.test.ts:27–39 verifies all required entries present.
Test imetaTag.test.ts:57–68 verifies round-trip: buildImetaTag(attachment, 'full') → parseMediaImetaTag() recovers url, sha256, type, filename, nonce, version with exact equality.

buildImetaTag at imetaTag.ts:23 pushes `role ${role}` directly. Test imetaTag.test.ts:41–48 verify 'role full' for full and 'role thumb' for thumb. Test imetaTag.test.ts:85–91 verify role entries preserved.

buildImageMessageContent at imageMessage.ts:9–12 returns JSON.stringify({ type: 'image', version: 1, caption }). Test imageMessage.test.ts:25–36 verifies JSON.parse round-trip produces correct structure.

Test imageMessage.test.ts:32–36 verifies buildImageMessageContent('') produces caption: ''. Test lines 46–50 verify parseImageMessageContent round-trip returns { type: 'image', version: 1, caption: '' }.

parseImageMessageContent at imageMessage.ts:14–33 returns null for: non-JSON (catch), type !== 'image', missing version. Tests imageMessage.test.ts:52–80 verify all null cases: non-JSON (52), wrong type (56), missing version (60), poll_open (64), poll_close (69), empty string (74), null JSON (78).

extractAttachmentsByRole at imageMessage.ts:52–57 reads 'role ' entry from tag. Line 66 extracts thumb, lines 69–70 treat missing/other as full per spec. Test imageMessage.test.ts:93–99 verifies both roles extracted correctly.
24 unit tests pass (8 imetaTag + 16 imageMessage) in Node environment with no browser API stubs required. Test output: 24 passed (0 failed).
Imports at imetaTag.ts:1 and imageMessage.ts:1 use '@internet-privacy/marmot-ts' root, not subpaths. Complies with exports map.
imetaTag.ts:3–26 is hand-rolled NIP-92 serializer (no applesauce-common dependency). Builds tag array manually per spec.

codex_findings: Codex unavailable (disable-model-invocation). Manual verification: all 10 questions pass. No critical/high issues. Note: extractAttachmentsByRole uses lazy require() to avoid circular deps (acceptable pattern). Module-scoped _parseTagImpl caching is appropriate for production.
